liftedFindings.title: examples/minimal-podcast is missing the authored files its own manifest and comments require
The production resolver never wires the new git tracking check
Freshness never notices an input REMOVED from the manifest, so a producer whose input list shrank reports `fresh`
Case 5 cannot discriminate record-based from disk-based input resolution — the property it claims to prove is untestable as written
`follows` can point at an unreachable catalogue target that is not a graph node
`src/cli/index.ts` self-executes at import time while also exporting `createProgram`/`run`
Provider output paths can escape `output_dir`
`loadProfile` joins an unvalidated profile name into a path, so a profile name with separators escapes the search-dir contract
Manifest-authored paths can escape the episode directory
Shipped `pc status` path is not checked because `src/cli/index.ts` is excluded
`assessRelease` is never tested against the `invalid` state its own sibling test proves is reachable
No coverage for a `follows` node with no `reviews` entry — the fresh-install baseline
Importing every emitted `.js` includes the CLI entry point, whose top-level side effects run inside the vitest worker
An ingest that has already overwritten `dist/` is not rolled back when the record write fails, leaving the ledger's hash and the file on disk disagreeing
`BuildOutputSchema.path` accepts any non-empty string, so a provider-declared `../` output escapes `dist/` at ingest
Provider outputs can escape `dist/` and be recorded as legitimate artifacts
Impure declarations can still carry an empty reason
Docker-absent is a permanently green skip: FR-027's only real proof can go unexecuted forever with no gate
Fetched asset bytes are handed to the provider under a *claimed* hash, never an observed one — breaking the module's own stated invariant
The execution ledger records 78/78 `reviewClean: true`, which the audited commit history directly contradicts
The cycle test's "names the offending declaration" assertions (`/a/`, `/b/`, `/c/`) match essentially any prose
`pc asset add` leaves the original bytes in place
`visit()` halts on any `needs-review` node, but its own contract says the rule is for AUTHORED nodes only
Derived explanations report every input as causal, including unrelated fresh branches
Content-addressed destination is written non-atomically, so a concurrent resolve can hand a provider a truncated file
Tracked large authored files are still refused in production paths
Fetched asset inputs lose their declared file type at the provider boundary
`cachedStore.has` is not covered by the cache-integrity contract
Release becomes falsely clean when a followed node is deleted
An authored node whose followed node is missing is reported as `absent` — a claim about the wrong file
`follows` can resolve a graph-valid identity that `resolveStatus` omitted
